section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following is a correct practice when welding in a confined space for critical work?
option_b: Use forced ventilation and gas monitoring
explanation: B correct: Forced ventilation and gas monitoring are essential in confined spaces. No ventilation, no permit, and welding alone are unsafe.

question_text: Which of the following best describes ?welding supervisor? for a large project?
option_b: Oversee safety, quality, and scheduling
explanation: B correct: Supervisors coordinate work and ensure standards. Doing all the welding, replacing the welder, and only inspecting are incomplete.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?plasma arc gouging? for repair work?
option_b: Removing metal from the surface in a groove
explanation: B correct: Plasma gouging removes metal in a groove for repair or preparation. Cutting completely, welding only, and grinding only are different.

question_text: Which of the following best describes ?backstep? welding technique in SMAW on thick sections?
option_b: Breaking a long weld into short segments welded in reverse order
explanation: B correct: Backstep reduces distortion and residual stress by breaking the weld into short segments. Continuous long passes increase distortion; no weaving and only flat are unrelated.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the main advantage of plasma arc cutting over oxy-fuel cutting for thick non-ferrous metals?
option_b: Better for thick, non-ferrous metals
explanation: B correct: Plasma cutting is good for thick, non-ferrous metals and offers precision. Slower cutting, no electricity, and no precision are incorrect.

question_text: What is the main effect of high carbon equivalent in steel?
option_b: Harder and more crack-sensitive
explanation: B correct: High CE steels are harder and more crack-sensitive. Easier to weld, softer, and less distortion are incorrect.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the main purpose of using a square in advanced layout?
option_b: Check for 90ｰ angles
explanation: B correct: A square checks for 90ｰ angles. Measuring temperature, current, and gas flow are done with other tools.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?confined space? in advanced welding?
option_b: Area with limited entry/exit and ventilation
explanation: B correct: A confined space has limited entry/exit and ventilation. A large open shop, no hazards, and only electrical hazards are incorrect.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the main advantage of FCAW-self-shielded over GMAW in windy outdoor conditions?
option_b: Better tolerance to wind and drafts
explanation: B correct: FCAW-self-shielded is less sensitive to wind than GMAW. Lower deposition, no shielding gas, and no fume are incorrect.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?submerged arc welding? (SAW) for thick sections?
option_a: Welding under a layer of flux
option_b: Welding with no shielding
explanation: A correct: SAW uses a granular flux layer over the weld. Welding with no shielding, only CO2, or only argon are different processes.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?visual inspection? in high-integrity welding?
option_b: Inspecting with the naked eye or magnification
explanation: B correct: Visual inspection uses the eye or magnification. Instruments only, only NDT, and only destructive tests are different.

question_text: What is the main purpose of a ?welding quality manual? for a fabrication shop?
option_b: Describe the company?s quality system for welding
explanation: B correct: Quality manuals define how quality is managed. Decorating the shop, replacing PPE, and replacing inspection are incorrect.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?acceptance criteria? in high-integrity welding?
option_b: Standards for what is acceptable in a weld
explanation: B correct: Acceptance criteria define what is acceptable in a weld. Rejecting all welds, no standards, and only visual standards are incorrect.

question_text: Which of the following best describes ?keyhole? welding in GTAW on thick stainless?
option_a: Deep penetration with a hole through the joint
option_b: Shallow penetration
explanation: A correct: Keyhole GTAW produces a deep, through-penetration hole in the joint. Shallow penetration, no penetration, and welding without filler are different.

question_text: Which of the following best describes ?porosity? in a high-integrity weld?
option_b: Gas pockets trapped in the weld
explanation: B correct: Porosity is gas bubbles that weaken the weld. Smooth, solid weld is good; extra reinforcement and type of electrode are different.

question_text: What is the correct way to handle a weld with excessive undercut in a critical joint?
option_b: Follow repair procedures
explanation: B correct: Excessive undercut must be repaired per procedure. Accepting it, welding over it without cleaning, and ignoring it are unsafe.

question_text: What is the correct way to handle a weld that is overwelded in a high-stress joint?
option_b: Follow repair procedures
explanation: B correct: Overwelded welds may need grinding or rework. Accepting it, welding over it again, and ignoring it are unsafe.

question_text: Which of the following best describes ?high-frequency? start in GTAW?
option_b: Touch-start only
explanation: A correct: High-frequency start initiates the arc without touching the workpiece. Touch-start, manual striking, and no gas needed are incorrect.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?material traceability? in high-integrity work?
option_a: Tracking where the material came from
option_b: Ignoring material source
explanation: A correct: Traceability tracks the material?s origin and history. Ignoring source, using any material, and no documentation are incorrect.

question_text: Which of the following best describes ?welding procedure specification? (WPS) for critical welds?
option_b: Provide approved welding parameters
explanation: B correct: A WPS gives approved parameters and methods. Decorating the shop, replacing PPE, and replacing inspection are incorrect.

question_text: Which tungsten electrode is best for DC GTAW on stainless steel?
option_b: Thoriated
explanation: B correct: Thoriated tungsten is commonly used for DC GTAW on stainless. Pure tungsten is for AC aluminum; ceriated and zirconiated are alternatives.

question_text: Which of the following best describes ?underbead cracking? in SMAW?
option_b: Crack in the heat-affected zone just below the weld
option_d: Crack in the base metal far from the weld
explanation: B correct: Underbead cracking occurs in the HAZ below the weld, often due to hydrogen and stress. Cracks in the weld metal, on the surface, or far from the weld are different.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the main advantage of MCAW over GMAW for thick sections?
option_b: Higher deposition and better penetration
explanation: B correct: MCAW often has higher deposition and better penetration than GMAW. Lower deposition, no shielding gas, and no fume are incorrect.

question_text: What is the main purpose of estimating in advanced welding projects?
option_b: Predict material, time, and cost for complex jobs
explanation: B correct: Estimating predicts material, time, and cost for a job. Reducing safety, replacing inspection, and increasing distortion are incorrect.

question_text: What is the correct way to handle a weld with clustered porosity in a critical application?
option_b: Follow repair procedures
explanation: B correct: Clustered porosity reduces strength and must be repaired. Accepting it, welding over it without cleaning, and ignoring it are unsafe.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?heat number? on a material for critical work?
option_a: Number of welds on the piece
option_b: Number assigned to a batch of material
explanation: B correct: A heat number identifies a batch of material for traceability. Number of welds, hours worked, and inspectors are incorrect.

question_text: What is the correct way to handle a weld with delayed cracking in a high-integrity structure?
option_b: Investigate and correct the cause
explanation: B correct: Delayed cracking must be investigated and corrected. Accepting it, welding over it without cleaning, and ignoring it are unsafe.

question_text: Which of the following best describes ?welding training record? for advanced welders?
option_b: Document skills and qualifications
explanation: B correct: Training records show competency. Decorating the shop, replacing PPE, and replacing inspection are incorrect.

question_text: What is the correct way to stop a GTAW weld on stainless without a crater crack?
option_b: Back-fill the crater with filler metal
option_d: Use higher amperage at the end
explanation: B correct: Back-filling the crater with filler metal reduces shrinkage stress. Pulling straight off, stopping abruptly, and using higher amperage increase cracking risk.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the main advantage of plasma gouging over air-carbon arc gouging?
option_b: Less fume and cleaner groove
explanation: B correct: Plasma gouging produces less fume and a cleaner groove. More fume, no electricity, and no PPE are incorrect.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the main purpose of a weld procedure data sheet (WPS) for critical welds?
option_b: Provide approved welding parameters
explanation: B correct: A WPS gives approved parameters and methods. Decorating the shop, replacing PPE, and replacing inspection are incorrect.

question_text: Which of the following best describes ?heat-affected zone? (HAZ) in high-strength steel?
option_b: Area of the base metal affected by heat but not melted
explanation: B correct: The HAZ is the base metal heated by the weld but not melted. Where the electrode melts is the weld metal; slag and shielding gas are different.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?flux-cored arc welding? (FCAW) for outdoor structural work?
option_a: Uses a solid wire with external gas
option_b: Uses a tubular wire with flux inside
explanation: B correct: FCAW uses a tubular wire with flux inside. Solid wire with external gas is GMAW; no wire or GTAW wire are incorrect.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the main purpose of a weld gauge for critical welds?
option_b: Measure weld dimensions and profiles
explanation: B correct: A weld gauge measures weld size, reinforcement, undercut, etc. Measuring temperature, current, and gas flow are done with other tools.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?mill test report? (MTR) for high-strength steel?
option_b: A report on the base metal properties
explanation: B correct: An MTR documents the base metal?s chemical and mechanical properties. A test on the weld, a safety inspection, and a training record are different.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?plasma arc cutting? for thick non-ferrous metal?
option_b: Cutting with only oxy-fuel
explanation: A correct: Plasma arc cutting uses a constricted arc to cut metal. Oxy-fuel, grinding, and sawing are different methods.

question_text: What is the main disadvantage of GTAW compared to SMAW for production work?
option_b: No precision
explanation: A correct: GTAW is more expensive and slower than SMAW. No precision, no shielding gas, and more fume are incorrect.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the correct way to handle a material that does not match the drawing for a critical weld?
option_b: Report it and follow procedures
explanation: B correct: Non-conforming material must be reported and handled per procedure. Using it anyway, welding over it, and ignoring the drawing are unsafe.

question_text: What is the main purpose of post-weld heat treatment (PWHT) on high-strength steel welds?
option_b: Relieve residual stress and improve toughness
explanation: B correct: PWHT relieves stress and improves toughness. Increasing hardness, increasing spatter, and reducing PPE are incorrect.

question_text: What is the main purpose of post-weld heat treatment (PWHT) on thick SMAW welds?
option_b: Relieve residual stress and improve toughness
explanation: B correct: PWHT relieves stress and improves toughness. Increasing hardness, increasing spatter, and reducing PPE are incorrect.

question_text: What is the correct way to handle a layout error on a complex assembly?
option_b: Correct it before cutting or welding
explanation: B correct: Layout errors must be corrected before cutting or welding to avoid waste. Ignoring it, cutting anyway, or welding over it are unsafe.

question_text: What is the main purpose of a ?welding log? for high-integrity work?
option_b: Record welds, settings, and inspections
explanation: B correct: Logs provide traceability and quality records. Replacing inspection, decorating the shop, and replacing PPE are incorrect.

question_text: What is the main purpose of preheat and interpass temperature control when welding thick HSLA steel with SMAW?
option_b: Reduce hydrogen-induced cracking and control cooling rate
explanation: B correct: Preheat and interpass temperature slow cooling and reduce cracking risk. Increasing speed, improving appearance, and reducing spatter are secondary.

question_text: What is the correct way to extinguish a SMAW electrode that has gone out during a critical weld?
option_a: Relight it immediately in the crater
option_b: Break off the stub and start a new electrode
explanation: B correct: Broken or extinguished electrodes should be replaced; relighting in the crater can trap slag and gas. Continuing with the stub or using it for grinding is unsafe.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the correct way to handle a weld that fails NDT in a critical application?
option_b: Follow repair procedures
explanation: B correct: Failed welds must be repaired per procedure. Accepting it, welding over it without cleaning, and ignoring the report are unsafe.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?scale drawing? in advanced fabrication?
option_b: Drawing smaller or larger than actual size
explanation: B correct: Scale drawings are smaller or larger than actual size. Drawing at actual size is full-size; no measurements or only for fun are incorrect.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the correct way to handle a layout that is out of tolerance on a critical assembly?
option_b: Correct it before cutting or welding
explanation: B correct: Layouts out of tolerance must be corrected before cutting or welding. Ignoring it, cutting anyway, and welding over it are unsafe.

question_text: Which of the following best describes ?weld cracking? in a critical joint?
option_b: Break in the weld or base metal
explanation: B correct: Cracks are serious defects that weaken the joint. Smooth joint is good; extra metal and type of electrode are different.

question_text: Which of the following best describes ?overwelding? in critical work?
option_b: Excessive weld metal that wastes material
explanation: B correct: Overwelding adds unnecessary metal and heat. Too little is underwelding; perfect size is correct; type of electrode is wrong.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the main purpose of using a protractor in advanced layout?
option_b: Measure angles
explanation: B correct: A protractor measures angles for layout. Measuring temperature, current, and gas flow are done with other tools.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?circumference? of a pipe for layout?
option_b: Distance around the pipe
explanation: B correct: Circumference is the distance around the pipe. Diameter, thickness, and length are different measurements.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the main purpose of a hot work permit in high-integrity welding?
option_b: Control fire risk during welding
explanation: B correct: Hot work permits control fire risk. Decorating the shop, replacing PPE, and replacing inspection are incorrect.

question_text: Which gas mixture is most commonly used for GTAW on stainless steel?
option_b: Argon-helium
explanation: D correct: Argon with 1?2% oxygen is common for stainless GTAW to improve arc stability and wetting. Pure argon is for aluminum; argon-helium for thicker metals; argon-CO2 is for GMAW.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the main advantage of SAW for thick-section welding?
option_b: High deposition rate and minimal fume
explanation: B correct: SAW has high deposition and low fume. Very low deposition, no flux, and only for thin metal are incorrect.

question_text: What is the main purpose of a ?welding safety meeting? for high-risk work?
option_b: Review hazards, procedures, and incidents
explanation: B correct: Safety meetings reinforce safe work practices. Decorating the shop, replacing PPE, and replacing inspection are incorrect.

question_text: Which of the following best describes ?layout? in advanced fabrication?
option_b: Measuring and marking parts for complex assemblies
explanation: B correct: Layout is the measuring and marking of parts before cutting or welding. Final welding, cleaning only, and grinding are different steps.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the main purpose of a weld procedure qualification record (WPQR) for critical welds?
option_b: Prove a WPS produces acceptable welds
explanation: B correct: A WPQR proves a WPS is qualified. Decorating the shop, replacing inspection, and replacing PPE are incorrect.

question_text: Which of the following best describes ?stringer bead? technique in SMAW on thick sections?
option_b: No weaving, straight bead
explanation: B correct: Stringer beads are straight, no-weave passes, often used for root and hot passes on thick sections. Wide weaving, circular motion, and side-to-side describe weaving techniques.

question_text: What is the main purpose of a back purge in GTAW pipe welding on stainless steel?
option_b: Prevent oxidation on the root side
explanation: B correct: Back purge uses inert gas on the inside of the pipe to prevent oxidation. Reducing spatter, increasing travel speed, and reducing amperage are not its purpose.

question_text: Which of the following best describes ?welding codebook? for critical work?
option_b: Provide reference for standards and requirements
explanation: B correct: Codebooks contain rules and tables for welding. Decorating the shop, replacing PPE, and replacing inspection are incorrect.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the main hazard of welding in a confined space for high-integrity work?
option_b: Asphyxiation or toxic fumes
explanation: B correct: Confined spaces can trap fumes or displace oxygen. No hazard, only electric shock, and only fire are incomplete.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?lockout/tagout? in advanced welding?
option_b: Isolating energy sources before maintenance
explanation: B correct: Lockout/tagout isolates energy sources before maintenance. Locking the welding machine only, ignoring safety, and only for inspection are incomplete.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the main purpose of a weld inspection report for high-integrity work?
option_b: Document inspection results and compliance
explanation: B correct: Inspection reports document results and compliance. Decorating the shop, replacing PPE, and replacing welding are incorrect.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the correct way to handle a welding machine that smells like burning in a critical shop?
option_b: Turn it off and report it
explanation: B correct: A burning smell can indicate electrical or insulation failure. Keeping it running, spraying with water, and adding oil are unsafe.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the correct way to convert inches to millimeters for precision layout?
option_b: Divide by 2.54
explanation: A correct: Multiply inches by 2.54 to get millimeters. Dividing, adding, or subtracting are incorrect.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?metal-cored arc welding? (MCAW) for high-productivity work?
option_a: Uses a solid wire with flux coating
option_b: Uses a tubular wire with metal powder
explanation: B correct: MCAW uses a tubular wire filled with metal powder. Solid wire with flux coating is SMAW; no flux and only GTAW wire are incorrect.

question_text: Which of the following is a correct practice when marking out a complex pattern on plate?
option_b: Use a scribe or layout dye
explanation: B correct: Layout dye or scribe provides clear, accurate marks. Using any marker, crayon only, or ignoring measurements reduces accuracy.

question_text: What is the correct way to control lamellar tearing when welding thick plate with SMAW?
option_b: Use controlled heat input and proper joint design
explanation: B correct: Lamellar tearing is reduced by controlled heat input and proper joint design (e.g., double-bevel). Higher amperage, ignoring plate quality, and using any electrode increase risk.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?tolerance? in high-precision welding?
option_b: Allowable variation from the specified dimension
explanation: B correct: Tolerance is the allowable variation from the specified dimension. Exact only, no variation, and only for inspection are incorrect.

question_text: What is the main advantage of GTAW over SMAW for thin stainless steel?
option_b: Greater precision and low heat input
explanation: B correct: GTAW offers precise, low-heat input control, ideal for thin stainless. Higher deposition rate is SMAW; lower cost and no shielding gas are incorrect.

question_text: What is the main purpose of a ?welding audit? for high-integrity work?
option_b: Check that procedures and records are followed
explanation: B correct: Audits verify compliance with systems. Decorating the shop, replacing PPE, and replacing inspection are incorrect.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?radius? in advanced layout?
option_b: Distance from center to edge of a circle
explanation: B correct: Radius is the distance from the center to the edge of a circle. Straight line, thickness, and length are different.

question_text: What is the main purpose of pattern development in advanced fabrication?
option_b: Create accurate templates for complex cutting and bending
explanation: B correct: Pattern development creates accurate templates for layout and fabrication. Decorating the shop, replacing welding, and reducing PPE are incorrect.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?fume extraction? in high-production welding?
option_a: Removing fumes from the breathing zone
option_b: Adding fumes to the air
explanation: A correct: Fume extraction removes fumes from the breathing zone. Adding fumes, ignoring fumes, and only for outdoor are incorrect.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?arc eye? in advanced welding?
option_b: Skin burn
explanation: A correct: Arc eye is UV-induced eye pain. Skin burn, hearing loss, and muscle cramp are different injuries.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?non-destructive testing? (NDT) for critical welds?
option_a: Destroying the weld to test it
option_b: Testing the weld without damage
explanation: B correct: NDT inspects the weld without destroying it. Destroying the weld is destructive testing; only visual or only destructive are incomplete.

question_text: What is the correct way to start a GTAW arc using lift-arc start on stainless steel?
option_a: Touch the tungsten to the workpiece and lift
option_b: Use a high-frequency starter only
option_d: Hold the tungsten close and wait
explanation: A correct: Lift-arc start involves touching the tungsten and lifting to initiate the arc. High-frequency is another method; striking like a match or holding close are incorrect.

question_text: Which of the following is a correct practice when using a GTAW filler rod on stainless steel?
option_a: Dip the rod into the arc
option_b: Hold the rod in the arc continuously
option_c: Keep the rod outside the arc and feed as needed
option_d: Use the rod as an electrode
explanation: C correct: The filler rod should be kept outside the arc and fed into the leading edge of the puddle. Dipping into the arc, holding it in the arc, or using it as an electrode are incorrect.

question_text: Which of the following best describes ?carbon equivalent? (CE) in steel?
option_b: Formula combining carbon and alloy elements to predict weldability
explanation: B correct: CE combines carbon and alloy elements to predict hardenability and cracking risk. Total carbon only, oxygen, and hydrogen are not the definition.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?trade math? in advanced welding?
option_b: Math for layout, fit-up, and measurements on complex assemblies
explanation: B correct: Trade math is used for layout, fit-up, and measurements. Office work, painting only, and safety only are incorrect.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: Which of the following best describes ?undercut? in a high-integrity weld?
option_b: Groove melted into the base metal along the weld toe
explanation: B correct: Undercut is a groove that weakens the joint. Extra metal describes reinforcement; smooth transition is good; type of electrode is wrong.

question_text: What is the main advantage of using low-hydrogen electrodes (E7018) on thick, high-strength steel?
option_b: Reduced risk of hydrogen cracking
explanation: B correct: Low-hydrogen electrodes reduce hydrogen pickup and cracking risk. Lower cost, easier arc, and less fume are not their primary advantage.

question_text: Which of the following best describes ?welding quality plan? for critical projects?
option_b: Outline how quality will be achieved and verified
explanation: B correct: Quality plans define methods and checks. Decorating the shop, replacing PPE, and replacing inspection are incorrect.

question_text: Which of the following best describes ?preheating? in welding high-strength steel?
option_b: Cooling the workpiece after welding
explanation: A correct: Preheating raises the base metal temperature before welding to reduce cracking. Cooling after welding is postheat; heating the electrode or gas is incorrect.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the main purpose of using a divider in advanced layout?
option_b: Transfer measurements and mark arcs
explanation: B correct: Dividers transfer measurements and mark arcs. Measuring temperature, current, and gas flow are done with other tools.

question_text: Which of the following is a correct practice when storing E7018 electrodes for critical work?
option_b: Store them in a heated electrode oven at the recommended temperature
explanation: B correct: E7018 electrodes must be kept dry in a heated oven. Open air, the floor, and direct sunlight expose them to moisture.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the correct way to protect against arc eye in high-intensity welding?
option_b: Welding helmet with correct shade
explanation: B correct: A welding helmet with the correct shade blocks UV/IR. No protection, sunglasses, and regular safety glasses are insufficient.

question_text: Which SMAW electrode is best for welding high-strength low-alloy (HSLA) steel in all positions?
explanation: C correct: E7018 is low-hydrogen and suitable for HSLA and all-position work. E6010/13 are general-purpose; E7024 is flat/horizontal only.

section_name: WELDS ON MILD STEEL PLATE AND PIPE
question_text: What is the main purpose of a fire watch during high-intensity welding?
option_b: Monitor for sparks and fires after welding
explanation: B correct: A fire watch monitors for sparks and fires. Starting fires, replacing the welder, and replacing PPE are incorrect.
